persona.name: Conversational Spanish Speaker
persona.description: Someone who practices a little every day, and is no longer scared of the first sentence.

milestones.title: Hold a 5-minute conversation
milestones._comment: 20 of 21 — checking this off during the take completes it on camera

action.title: Practice speaking for 10 minutes
action.anchorLink: After I pour my morning coffee
action.kickstartVersion: Say three sentences out loud — that's it

action.title: Review 10 new words
action.anchorLink: After I sit down on the train

action.title: Narrate one thing I'm doing, in Spanish
action.kickstartVersion: Name three things in the kitchen

_comment_history: missDaysAgo = every action missed that day (red on the calendar — proves 'a missed day never erases you'). partialDaysAgo = only the first scheduled action done (amber). Today is deliberately left unlogged so the demo can complete it on camera.

notes.6: Nearly skipped it — did the short version instead

reflections.userInput: I'm ready for my weekly review.
reflections.aiFeedback: You showed up on four of five days last week — that's the Conversational Spanish Speaker doing exactly what they do. Let's protect what's working.

reflections.userInput: I'm ready for my weekly review.
reflections.aiFeedback: You showed up on four of five days last week — that's the Conversational Spanish Speaker doing exactly what they do. Let's protect what's working.

onboardingAnswers: I've been 'learning Spanish' for three years and still can't hold a conversation.
Ten or fifteen minutes a day is realistic. An hour is a fantasy.
